what_it_does: A canvas brush-mask editor that lets the user paint a region on top of a source image. It maintains a hidden mask canvas and a visible composed canvas; brushing erases mask alpha to mark 'edit here', shows the marked area as a gold tint, and emits an onChange(blob) PNG where transparent pixels mark the area to edit (per OpenAI gpt-image-2 images/edits spec). Tools: brush, eraser, brush size, invert, reset.
observations: Three canvases via refs: imgRef (offscreen Image), maskRef (hidden mask), composedRef (visible composite). Mask sized to image natural dimensions.

repaintComposed() reads mask alpha pixel-by-pixel; where alpha < 128 it tints gold (#E0C010 / rgba 224,192,16,180) and, for output, builds a canvas with RGB=source and alpha=0 where 'edit here' -> emits via out.toBlob PNG.
Brush uses globalCompositeOperation 'destination-out' (erases alpha); eraser uses 'source-over' (restores opacity). Mask initialized fully opaque (default = no edits).
Pointer handling scales client coords into mask pixel space (rect ratio), draws round-cap/round-join strokes between lastPos and current.
invert() inverts mask alpha (special-cased: if untouched/fully-opaque, clears to fully transparent so invert 'feels meaningful').
Toolbar buttons use CvIcon (edit, no-symbol) with tone switching on active state; help text explains the gold tint, and a status chip shows 'Mask ready' vs 'No mask yet'.
onChange is documented as debounced but is fired directly inside repaintComposed on every mask change (no visible debounce in this file).
standouts: Encodes a specific external API contract: alpha-transparent = region to edit, matching OpenAI gpt-image-2 images/edits — the mask semantics are inverted-alpha by design and the code is built around that.
Dual-canvas approach: hidden alpha mask drives both the gold-tint preview and the exported PNG from one source of truth.
conventions: Follows browser-React no-build pattern (suffixed hook aliases useState_me etc., window attach).
Hardcoded tint color (#E0C010 / 224,192,16) and rgba black strokes are canvas paint values, not CSS consumer tokens — outside the token system's scope.
concerns: Header comment says onChange is debounced, but the code fires onChange synchronously in repaintComposed on every stroke/repaint — getImageData + toBlob per move is potentially heavy and the documented debounce is absent (doc/behavior mismatch).
repaintComposed runs full-image getImageData and per-pixel loops on every pointer move, which can be expensive for large images (file-local performance smell).
notes: A brush-mask painter whose exported PNG follows OpenAI's edit-mask alpha convention (transparent = edit region); gold tint previews the mask, but the documented onChange debounce is not present.
questions: Is the missing debounce intentional, or should onChange be throttled given the per-move getImageData/toBlob cost?
Who consumes the emitted blob — is it MaskEditor's caller that pairs it with a prompt and sends to the openai-image provider?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly match — the editor and OpenAI-mask-alpha output match the header, but the stated 'debounced' onChange is not implemented as debounced.
apparent_themes: Conforming to an external AI provider's input contract (mask alpha semantics)
Single source (the alpha mask) driving both preview and export
intention_signals: Aims to give users an intuitive brush-on-the-image way to specify exactly which region an AI image edit should change, producing the precise alpha-PNG the provider requires.
novelty: Inverted-alpha mask painting purpose-built for the OpenAI images/edits contract, with a live gold-tint composite derived from the same mask canvas.
contribution: Contributes the inpainting mask-authoring surface — a brush/eraser canvas that produces the OpenAI-spec alpha-PNG mask and previews it as a gold tint.
summary_for_embedding: React canvas brush-mask editor producing an OpenAI gpt-image-2-spec alpha PNG (transparent = edit region), with brush/eraser/size/invert/reset and a gold-tint live preview.
intention_for_embedding: To let users paint the exact region an AI image edit should change, emitting the alpha-channel mask PNG the image provider requires.
